content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-autonomous agent framework) can be repurposed to prioritize what new information gets stored into an active learning agent's long-term memory, solving the 2024-2025 SOTA bottleneck of catastrophic forgetting and redundant memory bloat in open-world active learning agents. This creates a 'memories you earned via curiosity' system where only high-uncertainty, generalization-boosting information is retained, rather than all encountered unlabeled data.
